child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: contentType
child.description: Identifies the type of the data in the attachment and allows a method to be chosen to interpret or render the data. Includes mim...
child.longDesc: Identifies the type of the data in the attachment and allows a method to be chosen to interpret or render the data. Includes mime type parameters such as charset where appropriate.

child.name: language
child.description: The human language of the content. The value can be any valid value according to BCP 47.

child.name: data
child.description: The actual data of the attachment - a sequence of bytes, base64 encoded.

child.name: url
child.description: A location where the data can be accessed.

child.name: size
child.description: The number of bytes of data that make up this attachment (before base64 encoding, if that is done).

child.name: hash
child.description: The calculated hash of the data using SHA-1. Represented using base64.

child.name: title
child.description: A label or set of text to display in place of the data.

child.name: creation
child.description: The date that the attachment was first created.

child.name: height
child.description: Height of the image in pixels (photo/video).

child.name: width
child.description: Width of the image in pixels (photo/video).

child.name: frames
child.description: The number of frames in a photo. This is used with a multi-page fax, or an imaging acquisition context that takes multiple slice...
child.longDesc: The number of frames in a photo. This is used with a multi-page fax, or an imaging acquisition context that takes multiple slices in a single image, or an animated gif. If there is more than one frame, this SHALL have a value in order to alert interface software that a multi-frame capable rendering widget is required.

child.name: duration
child.description: The duration of the recording in seconds - for audio and video.

child.name: pages
child.description: The number of pages when printed.
